sections.paragraph:
διϊσχυρίζομαι impf. διϊσχυριζόμην to be emphatic or resolute about someth., insist, maintain firmly (so since Lysias, Isaeus, Pla.; Aelian, HA 7, 18; Cass. Dio 57, 23; PMich XIII, 659, 14 [VI a.d.]; Jos., Ant. 17, 336; Just., A I, 68, 8 [Hadrian]) Lk 22:59; Ac 15:2 D. W. inf. foll. (Jos., Ant. 2, 106) Ac 12:15.—New Docs 2, 81.—DELG s.v. ἰσχύς.

impf. impf. = imperfect

someth. someth. = something

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Isaeus Isaeus , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 246
